title: Wrapped Floer theory and Homological mirror symmetry for toric Calabi-Yau manifolds
speaker: Yoel Groman
abstract: Consider a Lagrangian torus fibration a la SYZ over a non compact base. Using techniques from arXiv:1510.04265, I will discuss the construction of wrapped Floer theory in this setting. Note that this setting is generally not exact even near infinity. The construction allows the formulation of a version of the homological mirror symmetry conjecture for open manifolds which are not exact near infinity.

proof_stages.label: Toric Calabi-Yau complement and Gross fibration
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines the open symplectic manifold X by starting with a toric Calabi-Yau M, using the product-of-coordinates map descending from symplectic reduction, and removing an anticanonical fiber so that X carries a projection to a cylinder with generic (C^*)^2 fibers, a toric singular fiber, residual torus action, and a Gross special Lagrangian torus fibration.

proof_stages.label: SYZ mirror as a moduli space of torus fibers
proof_stages.summary: The known SYZ construction is recalled: the mirror X-check is the moduli of Lagrangian torus fibers equipped with formal local systems, and it can be written explicitly as an affine/rigid space with equation xy=F(u,v), where F is a Laurent polynomial determined by the toric diagram.

proof_stages.label: Floer-theoretic statements implying HMS
proof_stages.summary: Assuming an appropriate wrapped Fukaya category can be defined, the speaker states that SH^0(X) is the algebra of functions on X-check, that a carefully chosen Lagrangian section is unobstructed with closed-open map an isomorphism, and that Abouzaid's generation criterion then gives the homological mirror symmetry equivalence with coherent sheaves on X-check.

proof_stages.label: Compactness problem on open manifolds
proof_stages.summary: To make the wrapped theory meaningful, the possible failures of Floer compactness are separated into pointlike/type-I escape and breaking-at-infinity/type-II escape, with type I ruled out by choosing Hamiltonians and almost complex structures of I-bounded geometry and using monotone homotopies to ensure invariance.

proof_stages.label: Type-II escape prevention by displacement at infinity
proof_stages.summary: The remaining type-II divergence is excluded by requiring that, outside a compact set, the time-one Hamiltonian flow moves every point a uniformly positive distance, a condition that becomes part of the admissible wrapping data.

proof_stages.label: Lipschitz Hamiltonians over the SYZ base
proof_stages.summary: For a Lagrangian torus fibration, Hamiltonians of the form H=h∘g with h Lipschitz in action-angle coordinates have controlled torus-direction flow, form a cofinal class satisfying the displacement condition, and define symplectic homology and wrapped Floer homology by direct limits.

proof_stages.label: HMS for the Lipschitz-wrapped category
proof_stages.summary: With wrapping defined via Lipschitz Hamiltonians, the final result is summarized as identifying this wrapped Fukaya category of X with the bounded derived category of coherent sheaves on the SYZ mirror.

examples.name: The basic case X from C^3
examples.note: The affine toric Calabi-Yau C^3 is used as the simplest mental model for the general toric Calabi-Yau setup and for the singular SYZ fiber/focus-focus-type degeneration.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker uses the C^3 case to trace the fibration picture, identifying the generic fiber as (C*)^2 and the singular fiber/product-zero locus with the simplest focus-focus type degeneration

passes.reason: The C^3-derived case is the minimal affine toric Calabi–Yau base case used as a representative model, not as an obstruction.

examples.name: Cotangent cylinder T^*S^1
examples.note: The toy computation of symplectic homology for T^*S^1 shows how increasing Hamiltonian slopes give winding periodic orbits and hence Laurent monomials, motivating the later Lipschitz/direct-limit definition of wrapping for SYZ fibrations.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker develops T^*S^1 as a toy model, describing its mirror, Hamiltonian flow, periodic orbits, direct-limit symplectic cohomology, Laurent polynomial ring, and wrapped Floer computation for a section

passes.reason: T^*S^1 is the lowest-dimensional cotangent-cylinder toy model for the wrapping computation, not a counterexample.
T^*S^1 is the minimal cotangent-cylinder toy model for the wrapping construction, not an adversarial example.
T^*S^1 is the flat one-dimensional cotangent-cylinder toy model for the wrapping computation.

rank-one cotangent cylinder with no singular SYZ features used as a minimal toy model
